ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Designs and develops furniture and interior spaces to meet aesthetic and functional requirements.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Furniture designers create prototypes, test, and refine designs to ensure they meet client needs, industry standards, and safety regulations. They work with various materials, including wood, metal, and plastics, and use computer-aided design (CAD) software to create detailed drawings and models.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: Diploma in Interior Design or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in design and creativity can pursue a diploma or certificate course in interior design or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue a certification or diploma course in furniture design to bridge the gap.

future_builder.description: Experienced designers can advance their skills by learning new software, materials, and techniques to stay competitive.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience as a Junior Designer

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into furniture design by acquiring relevant skills and building a portfolio.
portfolio_proof_required: Furniture design project with a functional prototype

roadmap.title: Capstone & Portfolio
topics: Furniture design project with a functional prototype
